text: At the end of a stretch of homes where Moonshadow's the restaurant once stood, you'll also see this: a block of seemingly untouched homes. Across the highway, a concrete rock wall above the K rail. But beyond that cement break, the destruction returns. By the time you get up to Dukes, much of the destruction ends. The fires able to be held here at Los Flores. Driving back home south on PCH, more of the same: miles and miles of devastation up against the Pacific Ocean. I notice in the distance the skyline, and realized is the first time downtown Santa Monica has been visible from this part of the highway in generations. It is still unclear how long it will be before people are allowed to rebuild their homes and their lives. Of course, the devastation is far-reaching beyond Malibu to the Palisades and Altadena. Ways that you can help: visit kta.com/lastrong. In the newsroom, Andy Reesmeyer, KTLA 5 News.
